summary: Partially decompiled the effect-library allocator/constructor path around efLib_8005BE88, adding C structure knowledge for Effect and EffectDesc, renaming the object allocator global from efLib_80458EB0 to Effect_AllocData, and renaming fn_8005BB40 to Effect_RemoveUserData. The PR also expanded eflib.h prototypes, forward declarations, symbol metadata, and debug allocator references so the new names are used consistently across C, asm, and GALE01 symbols. decomp.dev reported efLib_8005BE88 improved by +483 bytes to 59.61%, with a small +11 byte improvement for efLib_8005FCD8.
what_changed: Added a C implementation attempt for efLib_8005BE88 returning Effect* and taking int arg0 plus HSD_GObj* parent_gobj.
Changed efLib_8005FCD8 to call efLib_8005BE88(0, gobj), return Effect*, and use Effect field names instead of ef_UnkStruct0 fields.
Renamed the BSS HSD_ObjAllocData object at 0x80458EB0 from efLib_80458EB0 to Effect_AllocData in symbols, asm references, C declarations, and db allocator limiter code.
Renamed fn_8005BB40 at 0x8005BB40 to Effect_RemoveUserData and wired that symbol into GObj_InitUserData.
Promoted/replaced ef_UnkStruct0 with struct Effect in src/melee/ef/types.h, including gobj, parent_gobj, callback, and several byte/halfword fields through offset 0x2B.
Added struct EffectDesc with a float x0 and StaticModelDesc model_desc.
Added many eflib.h prototypes, mostly as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders, and added forward declarations for Effect, EffectDesc, and efLib_UnkCallback.
Added includes for baselib gobj/object/plink/proc/userdata/gxlink and lb_00F9 support needed by the new C code.
Updated GALE01 symbol metadata for eflib string/data objects, including string annotations, align:4 annotations, and splitting lbl_803BF80E out of efLib_803BF804.
Updated asm comments in asm/melee/ef/eflib.s with section offsets and function sizes while keeping large remaining portions in assembly.
smart_moves: Renaming efLib_80458EB0 to Effect_AllocData made the allocator's role clear and allowed all HSD_ObjAlloc/HSD_ObjFree/debug limiter references to converge on one semantic name.
Renaming fn_8005BB40 to Effect_RemoveUserData captured its observed role as the GObj user-data cleanup callback used by GObj_InitUserData.

The PR did not force full decompilation: unresolved logic such as EffectDesc lookup was left as commented M2C-style notes with desc set to NULL, preserving evidence without pretending the expression was solved.
Updating C, assembly labels, and symbols.txt together prevented cross-file symbol drift after the allocator and callback renames.
Adding forward declarations and broad eflib prototypes created safer call-site types for later effect-library work without needing every function to be decompiled immediately.
decomp_lessons: When a generic numbered struct starts to show stable behavior, replacing it with a semantic type like Effect can unlock better signatures and field documentation across callers.
Allocator globals are often worth naming early; HSD_ObjAllocData objects appear in construction, destruction, and debug-limit code, so a clear allocator name improves multiple subsystems at once.
For partial matches, it is acceptable to land a mixed state with TODO/commented M2C expressions where the hard pointer arithmetic is not yet solved, as long as the uncertainty is visible.
Callback names can be inferred from registration sites: the cleanup routine registered through GObj_InitUserData is stronger evidence for Effect_RemoveUserData than the raw fn_ address.
Symbol metadata cleanup, especially string sizes and alignment, can be part of decomp progress because it stabilizes data layout assumptions used by C matching.
naming_conventions: Use Effect for the recovered effect instance struct instead of ef_UnkStruct0.
Use EffectDesc for the recovered descriptor containing StaticModelDesc.
Use Effect_AllocData for the effect allocator HSD_ObjAllocData at 0x80458EB0.
Use Effect_RemoveUserData for the GObj user-data cleanup function at 0x8005BB40.
Keep unknown effect functions in the existing efLib_8005XXXX or fn_8005XXXX style until behavior is known.
Use explicit address comments in headers, for example /* 05BE88 */ Effect* efLib_8005BE88(...).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Kept remaining eflib.s assembly but changed labels to semantic names where the C and symbols were updated.
Added section offset/size comments to asm/melee/ef/eflib.s, such as .text:0x9D0 | 0x8005BE88 | size: 0x32C.
Used M2C_FIELD and M2C_CARRY expressions in the partial C code for difficult field/pointer/carry patterns rather than prematurely simplifying them.
Used nested scoped temporaries in efLib_8005BE88, likely to preserve or approximate the original control-flow/register structure during matching.
Updated db_2253.s labels from efLib_80458EB0 to Effect_AllocData so handwritten assembly stays symbolically aligned with C and symbols.txt.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 progress: main/melee/ef/eflib | efLib_8005BE88 +483 bytes -> 59.61%.
decomp.dev bot also reported main/melee/ef/eflib | efLib_8005FCD8 +11 bytes -> 47.69%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that complete or revise efLib_8005BE88 and resolve the EffectDesc lookup from efAsync_803C025C.
Find later PRs that rename Effect fields x0, x24, x26, x27, x28, x29, x2A, and x2B.
Find callers of efLib_8005BE88 after the signature changed to include parent_gobj.
Find later changes to Effect_RemoveUserData and fn_8005BC50 to confirm cleanup semantics.
Search for Effect_AllocData usage to validate allocator lifecycle and debug limiter behavior.
